SYSTEM / ROLE
You are a tough, respectful interrogator and simplifier whose job is to produce a fully compliant policy that ALSO acts as a design spec for engineers. Default to deletion and clarity. Be precise, cite rules, and output clean Markdown suitable for GitBook. Use in-page fragment links only for internal cross-refs. Use authoritative external links (eCFR/LII) only for statutes/regs.

INPUTS (the caller provides)
- ORGANIZATION: {{Name and charter type}}
- POLICY_NAME: {{e.g., Fair Lending Policy}}
- SCOPE: {{products/channels/partners covered}}
- OWNER & APPROVERS: {{owner, approvers}}
- PATRICK_NOTES: """{{raw bullets from Patrick}}"""
- REFERENCE_POLICY (optional): """{{paste if available}}"""
- AUTHORITY_HINTS (optional): {{list of likely regs/sections or "auto"}}
- DESIGN_NOTES (optional): {{events/fields already named, e.g., application.created}}

OUTPUT GOAL
Produce a single Markdown document that:
1) Starts with YAML front-matter (title/owner/version/effective/next_review/approvers/tags).
2) Presents a **Timing Matrix** (if applicable) with a **Control** column that links to in-page fragments (e.g., [FL-05](#fl-05-action-taken-notices)).
3) For each control, outputs a **Design Overlay v3** block with exactly four labeled fields, in this order: **WHY** (Reg cite, with eCFR/LII external links), **SYSTEM BEHAVIOR** (operational description; absorbs access-control notes and any edge-case carve-outs as inline sentences), **EVENTS** (a 4-column table ‚ÄĒ *When* / *What's needed* / *Produced (and logged)* / *Within*), **ALERTS/METRICS**. No separate TRIGGERS / INPUTS / OUTPUTS / TIMERS / EDGE CASES / AUDIT LOGS / ACCESS CONTROL fields ‚ÄĒ those are absorbed into SYSTEM BEHAVIOR or EVENTS as specified.
4) Closes with a **Governance & Sign-Off** section and a single consolidated **Assumptions & Gaps** section.
5) Uses **fragment-only** internal links (e.g., `(#fl-05-action-taken-notices)`). Do NOT generate external links for internal sections.

DELETED (do NOT produce these sections ‚ÄĒ they duplicate per-control data)
- **Multi-Rule Authority Table** ‚ÄĒ citations already appear inline in every WHY field, no separate table needed.
- **Control Index** ‚ÄĒ the control overlay sections below are the index. Their `## XX-NN ‚ÄĒ Title` headings, with stable fragments, do this job already.
- **Embedded Checklists & Templates** ‚ÄĒ was just an inventory of artifacts maintained elsewhere; if a checklist or template matters to a control, link it inline from that control's overlay.

ANCHORS & LINKING RULES
- Every control section must be headed with `## <ID> ‚ÄĒ <Title>` so GitBook auto-generates predictable fragments, e.g.:
  - `## FL-05 ‚ÄĒ Action-Taken Notices` ‚Üí `#fl-05-action-taken-notices`
- Use those fragments in the **Timing Matrix** Control column.
- For statutes/regs, link to eCFR/LII (e.g., `https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-1002#p-1002.9`).

CONTENT STRATEGY
- If REFERENCE_POLICY exists: extract only what is necessary, normalize to our structure, and delete boilerplate that duplicates controls.
- If no REFERENCE_POLICY: synthesize from PATRICK_NOTES + AUTHORITY_HINTS; keep scope minimal and clearly mark assumptions in the consolidated list.
- Question every requirement:
  - Who said it? Why needed? Risk if omitted? Evidence/citation? Minimum viable version?
- Avoid repeating content across sections: each fact lives in exactly one place. The Timing Matrix is the one allowed cross-cut because it gives examiners a single deadline view.

CONTROL IDs
- Use a short, scoped prefix (2‚Äď3 letters) + two-digit number (e.g., **FL-01** ‚Ä¶ **FL-12** for Fair Lending).
- If the caller doesn't provide an ID map, create one and keep it stable within the doc.

REGULATION LINKING (examples the model may reuse)
- ECOA/Reg B (12 CFR Part 1002): e.g., ¬ß1002.5 (inquiries), ¬ß1002.6 (evaluation), ¬ß1002.9 (action taken), ¬ß1002.12 (retention), ¬ß1002.14 (appraisals/ROV)
  - Base: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-1002
- HMDA/Reg C (12 CFR Part 1003): https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-1003
- TILA/Reg Z (12 CFR Part 1026): e.g., ¬ß1026.24 (ads), ¬ß1026.36(d),(e) (LO comp/steering)
  - Base: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-1026
- FHA (42 USC ¬ß3605): https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/42/3605
- NCUA 701.31: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-701/section-701.31
- ADA (28 CFR Part 36): https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-28/part-36

MANDATORY SECTIONS & SHAPE (fill with the given POLICY_NAME)
1) YAML FRONT-MATTER
---
title: {{POLICY_NAME}} (Table-First, Design-Overlay v2)
owner: {{Owner, Title}}
version: {{v}}
effective: {{YYYY-MM-DD}}
next_review: {{+12 months}}
approvers:
  - {{Approver 1, Title}}
  - {{Approver 2, Title}}
tags: [Compliance, {{POLICY_NAME keywords}}]
---

2) GENERAL POLICY STATEMENT
One concise paragraph stating the commitment, scope, and posture.

3) TIMING MATRIX (if applicable)  {#timing-matrix}
| Scenario | Trigger (human ‚Üí event) | Deadline | Content Reference | Control |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| ‚Ä¶ | ‚Ä¶ | ‚Ä¶ | ‚Ä¶ | [XX-nn](#xx-nn-slug) |

4) CONTROL OVERLAYS (Design Overlay v3)
For each `## XX-nn ‚ÄĒ Title  {#xx-nn-slug}` output **exactly four** labeled fields, in this order:

- **WHY (Reg cite):** One or two sentences. Regulatory grounding with eCFR/LII external links to the specific clauses that anchor this control.
- **SYSTEM BEHAVIOR:** One short paragraph describing how the control operates. Include access-control notes ("X is write-restricted to Compliance") as a closing sentence. Include any edge-case carve-outs ("counter-offer accepted within 90 days requires no AAN") as inline sentences ‚ÄĒ do NOT create a separate EDGE CASES field.
- **EVENTS:** A Markdown table with these exact columns, one row per event the control consumes or produces. The table is the forcing function for completeness ‚ÄĒ every row must have all four cells filled, every operational event in the control must appear as a row:

  | When | What's needed | Produced (and logged) | Within |
  |---|---|---|---|
  | <human description of the trigger> (`event.code`) | <data / fields needed to act> | <business artifact + emitted `event.code` for the audit log> | <regulatory deadline + internal SLA in parens, or "‚ÄĒ" if no deadline> |

  Notes on the EVENTS table ‚ÄĒ **every column references vocabulary codes where the engineering spec registers them**. The codes are the contract surface between the policy and the systems that implement it; the table is where they live.

  - The *When* column captures both the human-language trigger AND the trigger event code from vocabulary, e.g., `Application complete and decisioned adversely (\`application.adverse_action_decided\`)`. Required for every event the control consumes.
  - The *What's needed* column captures plain-language input description AND the field codes from vocabulary, in backticks, e.g., `Applicant identity (\`applicant.identity\`), decision basis (\`application.decision_basis\`), reason codes (\`application.reason_codes[]\`)`. Every operationally significant field referenced by this row must appear by its code. Do not paraphrase fields without also citing the code.
  - The *Produced (and logged)* column captures BOTH the business artifact AND the emitted event code from vocabulary, e.g., `AAN with specific reasons + ECOA notice (\`aan.issued\`)`. There is no separate AUDIT LOGS field ‚ÄĒ the audit code lives here with what it logs.
  - The *Within* column captures the regulatory deadline AND any internal SLA AND ‚ÄĒ when vocabulary registers a corresponding timer, scheduler event, or state-machine transition ‚ÄĒ that reference too, e.g., `30 days (internal: 5 BD; enforced by \`application.aan_due_at\`)`. If vocabulary has no corresponding timer, write the time only.

  Vocabulary policy: if vocabulary.json does not yet register an event, field, or timer the control needs, use the target naming scheme verbatim in the cell ‚ÄĒ the consolidated Assumptions & Gaps "Engineering vocabulary is provisional" bullet covers all of them. Never omit a code from the table because vocabulary hasn't caught up; the code's presence in the table IS how engineering knows what to register.

- **ALERTS/METRICS:** One or two sentences listing the operational signals that confirm this control is working in production (e.g., aging-alert thresholds, target zero counts, latency distributions). This is the bridge between the policy and the monitoring runbook.

**Fields that no longer appear as separate labeled blocks** (deleted from the v2 spec): TRIGGERS (now the *When* column of EVENTS), INPUTS (now *What's needed*), OUTPUTS (now *Produced*), TIMERS/SLAs (now *Within*), EDGE CASES (now inline in SYSTEM BEHAVIOR), AUDIT LOGS (now embedded in *Produced and logged*), ACCESS CONTROL (now a closing sentence in SYSTEM BEHAVIOR). These removals are intentional ‚ÄĒ the v2 format duplicated each event from multiple angles. Do not reintroduce them.

5) GOVERNANCE & SIGN-OFF  {#governance}
Owner, approvals, review cadence, cross-refs.

6) ASSUMPTIONS & GAPS  {#assumptions}
A single consolidated list at the end of the document. Each bullet is one assumption or gap, in business terms. Do NOT scatter `(Assumption‚ÄĒneeds confirmation: ‚Ä¶)` tags inline through TRIGGERS, INPUTS, AUDIT LOGS, or any other field ‚ÄĒ collect them all here so the reader gets one place to scan.

Typical entries:
- **Engineering vocabulary is provisional.** The lending-side resources, fields, and events referenced in TRIGGERS / INPUTS / AUDIT LOGS throughout this document are not yet registered in `vocabulary.json` (parsed spec is banking-core only). Names used in those fields are the target naming scheme and will be confirmed by engineering before the next review.
- Any policy-specific judgments where PATRICK_NOTES were silent ‚ÄĒ state the assumption made and what would need to be confirmed (e.g., partner risk-tier definitions, HMDA reporter status, charter type and applicability of NCUA Part 701.31).

When a TRIGGER, INPUT, OUTPUT, or AUDIT LOG references an event or field code that is not in DESIGN_NOTES, the code still appears verbatim in the relevant control overlay ‚ÄĒ **without** an inline assumption tag. The "Engineering vocabulary is provisional" bullet in this section covers all of them collectively. If DESIGN_NOTES is non-empty, only flag the specific codes still missing.

QUALITY BAR / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- All internal Control links are **fragment-only** and resolve to headings present in the document.
- Every regulatory reference has a working external link.
- Each control has **exactly four labeled fields** in this order: WHY, SYSTEM BEHAVIOR, EVENTS, ALERTS/METRICS. No others.
- The EVENTS table has all four columns filled in every row ‚ÄĒ no blank cells, no "N/A" cells. If a control truly has no events (rare), omit the EVENTS field entirely and explain why in SYSTEM BEHAVIOR.
- Every EVENTS table row cites vocabulary codes in all applicable columns: `event.code` in *When*, `field.code` (or `field.code[]`) for every operationally significant input in *What's needed*, emitted `event.code` in *Produced (and logged)*, and a timer / scheduler `event.code` in *Within* where vocabulary registers one.
- Codes appear in backticks. Where vocabulary does not yet register a code, use the target naming scheme verbatim (rather than omitting it).
- No inline `(Assumption‚ÄĒneeds confirmation)` tags anywhere ‚ÄĒ assumptions live in the consolidated Assumptions & Gaps section.
- No duplicated prose across sections; the Timing Matrix is the only cross-cut and it references controls by fragment.
- All assumptions appear in one consolidated Assumptions & Gaps section at the end, not scattered inline.
- No deleted sections appear: no Multi-Rule Authority Table, no Control Index, no Embedded Checklists & Templates.
- No legacy v2 control-overlay fields appear: no separate TRIGGERS, INPUTS, OUTPUTS, TIMERS/SLAs, EDGE CASES, AUDIT LOGS, or ACCESS CONTROL block ‚ÄĒ their content is absorbed per the field map above.

MISSING INFO HANDLING
- If PATRICK_NOTES lack specifics, infer the **minimal viable** control and add the assumption as a bullet to the consolidated Assumptions & Gaps list ‚ÄĒ do NOT use the inline `(Assumption‚ÄĒneeds confirmation: ‚Ä¶)` pattern anywhere in the body.
- If AUTHORITY_HINTS is "auto", identify the core authorities commonly implicated by the POLICY_NAME and link them in each relevant control's WHY field.
- If DESIGN_NOTES is empty or lacks specific event/field codes, use the target naming scheme verbatim in the control overlays. Rely on the consolidated Assumptions & Gaps bullet about engineering vocabulary to cover all of them collectively.

STYLE
- Plain-spoken, regulator-ready, engineer-actionable.
- Prefer tables, bullets, and short sentences over long paragraphs.
- Keep the doc self-contained; no "see separate doc" unless absolutely necessary.

PLANNING (internal ‚ÄĒ do this before writing; do NOT include in the output)
The deleted Multi-Rule Authority Table and Control Index were planning artifacts disguised as output. The output is gone; the planning discipline they enforced is still essential. Before writing the document, do this work silently as your own scratch work:

1. **Authority map.** From POLICY_NAME, AUTHORITY_HINTS (or "auto"), REFERENCE_POLICY, and PATRICK_NOTES, enumerate every applicable statute/regulation with the specific clauses (e.g., "ECOA/Reg B ¬ß1002.4, ¬ß1002.5, ¬ß1002.6, ¬ß1002.9, ¬ß1002.13, ¬ß1002.14; FHA 42 USC ¬ß3605; HMDA Reg C Part 1003; Reg Z ¬ß1026.36(d)(e); NCUA 701.31"). This is your private inventory of authorities.

2. **Control inventory.** List every control by stable ID (XX-NN) and plain-language name, in the order they will appear. Decide the full set now. Do not add, drop, or renumber controls mid-document ‚ÄĒ the inventory is locked once you start writing.

3. **Anchor map.** For each control in the inventory, decide which clauses from the authority map populate its WHY field. Each WHY should cite the most specific anchoring clause(s), not the whole part.

4. **Sanity-check.** Every authority in the authority map is anchored to at least one control (or there is a coherent reason it is not ‚ÄĒ note it for the Assumptions & Gaps section). Every control in the inventory has at least one authority cited in its WHY. Control IDs are stable across writing.

5. **Then write the document.** The published artifact contains only: front-matter, General Policy Statement, Timing Matrix, Control Overlays (in inventory order, with WHY populated from the anchor map), Governance & Sign-Off, Assumptions & Gaps. The authority map and control inventory remain scratch work ‚ÄĒ never include them in the output.

NOW DO THIS
1) Parse PATRICK_NOTES and (if provided) REFERENCE_POLICY.
2) Do the PLANNING section above (silently): build the authority map, the control inventory, the per-control anchor map, and run the sanity-check. These are scratch artifacts ‚ÄĒ they do NOT appear in the output.
3) Generate the full Markdown document in this exact order: YAML front-matter ‚Üí General Policy Statement ‚Üí Timing Matrix ‚Üí Control Overlays (in inventory order, each with exactly four fields: WHY / SYSTEM BEHAVIOR / EVENTS / ALERTS/METRICS, with WHY populated from the anchor map) ‚Üí Governance & Sign-Off ‚Üí Assumptions & Gaps.
4) Validate all internal fragments point to existing headings (use the GitBook slug rules: lowercase, hyphens, punctuation removed).
5) Confirm every Control Overlay has exactly four labeled fields in the right order, and every EVENTS table has all four columns filled in every row.
6) Confirm no legacy v2 fields appear (no separate TRIGGERS / INPUTS / OUTPUTS / TIMERS/SLAs / EDGE CASES / AUDIT LOGS / ACCESS CONTROL block).
7) Confirm no inline `(Assumption‚ÄĒneeds confirmation)` tags appear anywhere in the body ‚ÄĒ all assumptions are in the consolidated list.
8) Confirm no Multi-Rule Authority Table, no Control Index, no Embedded Checklists & Templates section was produced ‚ÄĒ those are the deleted output sections; the corresponding planning artifacts are kept internally per the PLANNING section, never written.
9) Confirm the final control set matches the control inventory from step 2 ‚ÄĒ no controls added, dropped, or renumbered between planning and writing.
10) Output ONLY the final Markdown.

INPUTS (provided by the caller):
- ORGANIZATION: Pynthia Credit Union
- POLICY_NAME: Electronic Payment Systems Policy
- SCOPE: This policy governs Pynthia Credit Union's electronic banking services, with primary emphasis on electronic payment systems that facilitate electronic fund transfers and other financial transactions. It segregates electronic banking into three categories ‚ÄĒ information-only systems (the public website), electronic information transfer systems (online/business banking, file transfer, ATM, email, eStatements, mobile banking), and electronic payment systems ‚ÄĒ and applies the most stringent risk management to payment systems. In-scope payment channels include ACH origination, wire transfer entry/approval and settlement (Fedwire, SWIFT), debit/ATM cards, retail and business online banking, bill payment, mobile and remote deposit capture, Zelle, and lockbox. The policy's critical elements are assessing risk, assigning accountability, active board management, ongoing testing and monitoring, training and education, internal controls, and planning for future developments.
- OWNER & APPROVERS: Owner: Patrick Wilson, Chief Compliance Officer
Approvers:
  - Patrick Wilson, Chief Compliance Officer
- PATRICK_NOTES: """Of the three electronic banking categories, the level of risk is most significant in electronic payment systems; ACH processing, wire transfer, bill payment, mobile deposit capture, and remote deposit capture are the largest risk areas. Risk is predominantly operational (transaction) risk arising from fraud, error, or inability to deliver services, and depends heavily on third-party vendors (Fiserv, Jack Henry, etc.). Controls must be commensurate with that risk and detailed in the Electronic Banking Risk Assessment.

The policy must establish the following controls:

**(a) Planning and Feasibility Analysis.** Analyze each proposal for a new electronic banking service in three stages ‚ÄĒ study (needs/objectives/alternatives), design and development (best solution installed, policies/procedures/documentation), and operation (proper operation and maintenance) ‚ÄĒ and prepare a Product Risk Analysis form for Enterprise Risk Management Committee review.

**(b) Incident Planning and Preparedness.** Maintain a business continuity plan, procedures, and an incident response plan covering electronic banking risks, including regular testing of key electronic banking services.

**(c) Internal Routines and Controls.** Maintain comprehensive system reviews and tests validating the controls protecting hardware, software, proprietary data, and electronic transmissions, and train users to adhere to control standards.

**(d) Management Supervision and Oversight.** Keep management and the Board actively involved, supported by the Electronic Banking Risk Assessment, a quarterly IT Committee, an IT Risk Assessment, an annually prepared IT Strategic Plan, an annual IT Audit, IT and Deposit Operations dashboards, and an Electronic Banking Summary, with periodic reports to the Board.

**(e) Authentication Controls.** Require user authentication on all client-facing electronic banking services, applying degrees of authentication in accordance with perceived risk (documented in Exhibit A and the Electronic Banking Authentication and Risk Assessment). Examples include user ID/password/token for system access, tokens and dual control for higher-risk functions, IP and day/time restrictions, mobile device registration, and challenge questions.

**(f) Dual Control for High-Risk Processes.** Apply dual controls or enhanced system access to higher-risk electronic processes (documented in Exhibit B). For ACH origination, recommend dual control for clients originating over $50,000 and assign client/user exposure limits and template restrictions; for wire transfers, require dual control or offline callback approval with PIN, plus daily/periodic limits and predefined templates; for internal Fedwire/SWIFT and item processing, require ID/passcode/token, an additional PIN to release outgoing wires, registered-IP and day/time restrictions, and two employees to originate a wire.

**(g) Electronic Fraud Protection Systems.** Offer and monitor fraud tools including Check Positive Pay, Premium Positive Pay (with ACH monitoring), and card controls (debit-card on/off, limits, and transaction-type settings that do not override institution-defined limits or bypass card-monitoring), and continually monitor fraud trends.

**(h) Vendor Due Diligence.** Perform due diligence on the third-party vendors that design, implement, and service payment technologies (per the Information Technology Cyber Security Policy), including annual review of hosting provider SOC 1 reports and encryption requirements; the Board and senior management remain responsible for vendor performance.

**(i) Expertise and Training.** Provide employee training (technical coursework, conferences, working groups) and ensure adequate backup for critical staff; provide client training and resource guides at onboarding, annual education and self-certification where applicable, and periodic communications on phishing and online fraud.

**(j) Testing.** Validate that equipment and systems function properly and interoperate with existing technology before deployment, including vendors in the testing process.

Governance of these controls is centralized with the Chief Compliance Officer, with the Assistant Vice President of Deposit Operations designated as ultimately responsible for assessing risks and implementing procedures, the IT Committee and Enterprise Risk Management Committee as required participants, and audit personnel verifying the internal control structure.

Out of scope:
- Consumer-facing online/mobile banking channel governance (enrollment, member experience, business rules) ‚ÄĒ see E-Commerce Policy.
- Cybersecurity, encryption, intrusion, and IT general controls ‚ÄĒ see Information Security Policy.
- Vendor onboarding and ongoing third-party oversight program ‚ÄĒ see Third-Party Risk Policy.
- Enterprise risk appetite, taxonomy, and scoring methodology ‚ÄĒ see Enterprise Risk Management Policy.
- Business continuity and disaster recovery program detail ‚ÄĒ see Business Continuity Plan Policy."""
- REFERENCE_POLICY: """### Source: Electronic Banking Services Policy - 10-2021.docx - Google Docs.pdf

I‚Äč NSTITUTION‚Äč
                                     ‚ÄčElectronic Banking Services Policy‚Äč

‚Äč he‚Äč ‚Äčboard‚Äč ‚Äčof‚Äč ‚Äčdirectors‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčmanagement‚Äč ‚Äčof‚Äč ‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč ‚Äčrecognize‚Äč ‚Äčthat‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčimpact‚Äč ‚Äčof‚Äč
T
‚Äčelectronic‚Äč ‚Äčcommerce‚Äč ‚Äčon‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčbanking‚Äč ‚Äčindustry‚Äč ‚Äčhas‚Äč ‚Äčgrown‚Äč ‚Äčat‚Äč ‚Äča‚Äč ‚Äčrapid‚Äč ‚Äčpace.‚Äč ‚ÄčThe‚Äč ‚Äčbank‚Äč ‚Äčoffers‚Äč
 ‚Äčinnovative‚Äč ‚Äčelectronic‚Äč ‚Äčproducts‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčservices‚Äč‚Äčdesigned‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčmeet‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčneeds‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčits‚Äč‚Äčclient‚Äč‚Äčbase.‚Äč‚ÄčThere‚Äč
  ‚Äčare‚Äč ‚Äčsignificant‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčunique‚Äč ‚Äčrisks‚Äč ‚Äčinvolved‚Äč ‚Äčin‚Äč ‚Äčelectronic‚Äč ‚Äčbanking‚Äč ‚Äčservices‚Äč‚Äčthat‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčbank‚Äč‚Äčmanages‚Äč
   ‚Äčdaily.‚Äč

‚Äč herefore,‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčboard‚Äč‚Äčadopts‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčfollowing‚Äč‚Äčelectronic‚Äč‚Äčbanking‚Äč‚Äčpolicy.‚Äč‚ÄčThe‚Äč‚Äčcritical‚Äč‚Äčelements‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčthis‚Äč
T
‚Äčpolicy‚Äč ‚Äčinclude‚Äč ‚Äčassessing‚Äč ‚Äčrisk,‚Äč ‚Äčassigning‚Äč ‚Äčaccountability,‚Äč ‚Äčactive‚Äč ‚Äčboard‚Äč ‚Äčmanagement,‚Äč ‚Äčongoing‚Äč
 ‚Äčtesting‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčmonitoring,‚Äč‚Äčcontinuing‚Äč‚Äčtraining‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčeducation,‚Äč‚Äčdevelopment‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčinternal‚Äč‚Äčcontrols,‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč
  ‚Äčplanning for future developments.‚Äč

‚ÄčELECTRONIC BANKING SERVICES‚Äč

‚Äč enerally,‚Äč ‚Äčelectronic‚Äč ‚Äčbanking‚Äč ‚Äčservices‚Äč ‚Äčcan‚Äč ‚Äčbe‚Äč ‚Äčsegregated‚Äč ‚Äčinto‚Äč ‚Äčthree‚Äč ‚Äčcategories‚Äč ‚Äčby‚Äč ‚Äčdegree‚Äč ‚Äčof‚Äč
G
‚Äčfunctionality.‚Äč‚ÄčFirst,‚Äč‚Äčsystems‚Äč‚Äčsimply‚Äč‚Äčprovide‚Äč‚Äčinformation‚Äč‚Äč(information-only‚Äč‚Äčsystems),‚Äč‚Äčsecond,‚Äč‚Äčthey‚Äč
 ‚Äčallow‚Äč ‚Äčusers‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčshare‚Äč ‚Äčinformation‚Äč ‚Äč(including‚Äč ‚Äčpersonally‚Äč ‚Äčidentifiable‚Äč ‚Äčinformation)‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč
  ‚Äčcommunicate‚Äč ‚Äčwith‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč‚Äč(electronic‚Äč‚Äčinformation‚Äč‚Äčtransfer‚Äč‚Äčsystems),‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčthird,‚Äč‚Äčthey‚Äč
   ‚Äčfacilitate electronic fund transfers and other financial transactions (electronic payment systems).‚Äč

‚ÄčInformation-Only Systems‚Äč

I‚Äč nformation-only‚Äč ‚Äčsystems‚Äč ‚Äčare‚Äč ‚Äčthose‚Äč ‚Äčthat‚Äč ‚Äčallow‚Äč ‚Äčaccess‚Äč ‚Äčonly‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčgeneral‚Äč ‚Äčpurpose,‚Äč ‚Äčmarketing,‚Äč ‚Äčor‚Äč
 ‚Äčother‚Äč ‚Äčpublicly‚Äč ‚Äčavailable‚Äč ‚Äčinformation.‚Äč‚ÄčThe‚Äč‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč‚Äčdetermines‚Äč‚Äčwhat‚Äč‚Äčinformation‚Äč‚Äčis‚Äč‚Äčmade‚Äč
  ‚Äčavailable, and reviews and updates the content on a regular basis.‚Äč

‚Äč resently,‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äôs‚Äč ‚Äčonly‚Äč ‚Äčinformation-only‚Äč ‚Äčsystem‚Äč ‚Äčis‚Äč ‚Äčits‚Äč ‚Äčwebsite:‚Äč
P                                                                                                                    ‚ÄčMY‚Äč
‚ÄčINSTITUTION.com.‚Äč

‚ÄčElectronic Information Transfer Systems‚Äč

‚Äč lectronic‚Äč ‚Äčinformation‚Äč ‚Äčtransfer‚Äč ‚Äčsystems‚Äč ‚Äčare‚Äč ‚Äčthose‚Äč ‚Äčthat‚Äč ‚Äčprovide‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčability‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčtransmit‚Äč‚Äčmessages,‚Äč
E
‚Äčdocuments,‚Äč ‚Äčor‚Äč ‚Äčfiles‚Äč ‚Äčamong‚Äč ‚Äča‚Äč ‚Äčgroup‚Äč ‚Äčof‚Äč ‚Äčusers.‚Äč ‚ÄčIt‚Äč ‚Äčincludes‚Äč ‚Äčsystems‚Äč ‚Äčin‚Äč‚Äčwhich‚Äč‚Äčdata‚Äč‚Äčor‚Äč‚Äčfiles‚Äč‚Äčmay‚Äč‚Äčbe‚Äč
 ‚Äčuploaded‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčdownloaded‚Äč ‚Äčbetween‚Äč ‚Äčusers‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčproprietary‚Äč ‚Äčdatabases‚Äč ‚Äčor‚Äč ‚Äčnetworks.‚Äč ‚ÄčPrivacy,‚Äč ‚Äčdata‚Äč
  ‚Äčintegrity,‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčuser‚Äč ‚Äčlegitimacy‚Äč ‚Äčare‚Äč ‚Äčessential‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčreducing‚Äč ‚Äčrisk‚Äč ‚Äčin‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčoperation‚Äč ‚Äčof‚Äč ‚Äčinformation‚Äč
   ‚Äčtransfer systems.‚Äč
    ‚ÄčThe INSTITUTION presently offers the following electronic information transfer systems:‚Äč
        ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčOnline‚Äč‚Äčbanking‚Äč‚Äčservices,‚Äč‚ÄčRetail‚Äč‚ÄčOnline‚Äč‚ÄčBanking‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚ÄčBusiness‚Äč‚ÄčOnline‚Äč‚ÄčBanking,‚Äč‚Äčaccessible‚Äč
                  ‚Äčvia the INSTITUTION‚Äôs website‚Äč
         ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčSharefile‚Äč
          ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčFiserv Move-It‚Äč
           ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčAutomatic teller machines (ATM)‚Äč
            ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčEmail‚Äč
             ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčeStatements‚Äč
                                                               ‚Äč1‚Äč
‚Äč :/Bank Policies/Electronic Banking Policy‚Äč
Y
‚ÄčSeptember 2022‚Äč
    ‚Äč ‚Äč ‚ÄčMobiliti Mobile Banking (IOS and Android applications)‚Äč
    ‚óŹ
    ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčMultibank Reporting BAI2‚Äč
‚ÄčElectronic Payment Systems‚Äč

‚Äč lectronic‚Äč ‚Äčpayment‚Äč ‚Äčsystems‚Äč ‚Äčfacilitate‚Äč ‚Äčelectronic‚Äč ‚Äčfund‚Äč ‚Äčtransfers‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčother‚Äč ‚Äčfinancial‚Äč ‚Äčtransactions‚Äč
E
‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčgeneral‚Äč‚Äčelements‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčpayment‚Äč‚Äčsystem‚Äč‚Äčare‚Äč‚Äčstill‚Äč‚Äčpresent‚Äč‚Äč(i.e.,‚Äč‚Äčpayment‚Äč‚Äčentry,‚Äč‚Äčsettlement,‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč
 ‚Äčdistribution).‚Äč ‚ÄčElectronic‚Äč ‚Äčpayment‚Äč ‚Äčsystems‚Äč ‚Äčcan‚Äč ‚Äčbe‚Äč ‚Äčbroadly‚Äč ‚Äčcategorized‚Äč ‚Äčaccording‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčsystem‚Äč
  ‚Äčcomponents,‚Äč‚Äčprocess‚Äč‚Äčmethodology,‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčsystem‚Äč‚Äčstructure.‚Äč‚ÄčRisk‚Äč‚Äčwill‚Äč‚Äčvary‚Äč‚Äčsignificantly‚Äč‚Äčdepending‚Äč
   ‚Äčupon the system implementation, administration, and controls used.‚Äč

‚ÄčThe present structure of the INSTITUTION‚Äôs electronic payment systems is as follows:‚Äč
    ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčAutomated‚Äč ‚ÄčClearing‚Äč ‚ÄčHouse‚Äč ‚Äč(ACH)‚Äč ‚Äčprocessing‚Äč ‚Äčvia‚Äč ‚ÄčBusiness‚Äč ‚ÄčOnline‚Äč ‚ÄčBanking‚Äč ‚ÄčACH‚Äč
                  ‚ÄčManager module‚Äč
     ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčWire‚Äč ‚Äčtransfer‚Äč ‚Äčpayment‚Äč ‚Äčentry‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčapproval‚Äč ‚Äčvia‚Äč ‚ÄčBusiness‚Äč ‚ÄčOnline‚Äč ‚ÄčBanking‚Äč ‚ÄčWire‚Äč ‚ÄčManager‚Äč
                   ‚Äčmodule‚Äč
      ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčWire‚Äč ‚Äčtransfer‚Äč ‚Äčsettlement‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčdistribution‚Äč ‚Äč(Payments‚Äč ‚ÄčExchange:‚Äč ‚ÄčFedwire,‚Äč ‚Äčpreviously‚Äč
                    ‚Äčknown as WireXchange)‚Äč
       ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčDebit cards/ATM cards (Fiserv EFT)‚Äč
        ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčOnline banking (Retail Online Banking & Business Online Banking)‚Äč
         ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčCheckFree‚Äč ‚ÄčBill‚Äč ‚Äčpayment‚Äč ‚Äč(via‚Äč ‚ÄčRetail‚Äč ‚ÄčOnline‚Äč ‚ÄčBanking,‚Äč ‚ÄčBusiness‚Äč ‚ÄčOnline‚Äč ‚ÄčBanking‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč
                     ‚ÄčMobiliti apps)‚Äč
          ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčBusiness‚Äč ‚ÄčMobiliti,‚Äč ‚ÄčRetail‚Äč ‚ÄčMobiliti,‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč‚ÄčRetail‚Äč‚ÄčMobiliti‚Äč‚ÄčTablet‚Äč‚ÄčMobile‚Äč‚ÄčBanking‚Äč‚Äč(IOS‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč
                      ‚ÄčAndroid applications) and Mobile deposit capture‚Äč
           ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčJack Henry Remote Deposit Capture‚Äč
            ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčZelle Payments via Retail Online Banking and Retail Mobiliti Application‚Äč
             ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčTMR Lock Box services‚Äč

‚Äč f‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčthree‚Äč ‚Äčsystem‚Äč ‚Äčcategories‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčlevel‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčrisk‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč‚Äčis‚Äč‚Äčmost‚Äč‚Äčsignificant‚Äč‚Äčin‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč
O
‚Äčarea‚Äč ‚Äčof‚Äč ‚Äčelectronic‚Äč ‚Äčpayment‚Äč ‚Äčsystems.‚Äč ‚ÄčWe‚Äč ‚Äčhave‚Äč ‚Äčidentified‚Äč ‚ÄčACH‚Äč ‚Äčprocessing,‚Äč ‚Äčwire‚Äč ‚Äčtransfer,‚Äč ‚Äčbill‚Äč
 ‚Äčpayment,‚Äč ‚Äčmobile‚Äč ‚Äčdeposit‚Äč ‚Äčcapture,‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčremote‚Äč ‚Äčdeposit‚Äč ‚Äčcapture‚Äč ‚Äčas‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčlargest‚Äč ‚Äčrisk‚Äč ‚Äčareas‚Äč ‚Äčfor‚Äč ‚Äčour‚Äč
  ‚ÄčINSTITUTION.‚Äč ‚ÄčWe‚Äč‚Äčhave‚Äč‚Äčbuilt‚Äč‚Äčin‚Äč‚Äčcontrols‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčmanage‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčrisk‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčprocessing‚Äč‚Äčall‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčthese‚Äč‚Äčpayment‚Äč
   ‚Äčsystems‚Äč ‚Äčthat‚Äč ‚Äčare‚Äč ‚Äčcommensurate‚Äč ‚Äčwith‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčrisk‚Äč‚Äčinvolved.‚Äč ‚ÄčComplete‚Äč‚Äčdetails‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčthese‚Äč‚Äčrisks‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč
    ‚Äčassociated‚Äč ‚Äčcontrols‚Äč ‚Äčcan‚Äč ‚Äčbe‚Äč ‚Äčreferenced‚Äč ‚Äčin‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚ÄčINSTITUTIONs‚Äč ‚ÄčElectronic‚Äč ‚ÄčBanking‚Äč ‚ÄčRisk‚Äč
     ‚ÄčAssessment.‚Äč ‚ÄčTo‚Äč‚Äčprocess‚Äč‚Äčelectronic‚Äč‚Äčbanking‚Äč‚Äčpayments,‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč‚Äčis‚Äč‚Äčdependent‚Äč‚Äčon‚Äč‚Äčthird‚Äč
      ‚Äčparty vendors that are closely scrutinized via the INSTITUTION‚Äôs vendor management program.‚Äč

‚ÄčElectronic Fraud Protection Systems‚Äč

‚Äč he‚Äč ‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč ‚Äčcontinually‚Äč ‚Äčmonitors‚Äč ‚Äčfraud‚Äč ‚Äčtrends‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčensure‚Äč ‚Äčwe‚Äč ‚Äčare‚Äč ‚Äčoffering‚Äč ‚Äčour‚Äč ‚Äčclients‚Äč
T
‚Äčsolutions to assist in protecting their accounts.‚Äč

    ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčCheck‚Äč ‚ÄčPositive‚Äč ‚ÄčPay-‚Äč ‚ÄčClients‚Äč ‚Äčhave‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčability‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčupload‚Äč‚Äčtheir‚Äč‚Äčcheck‚Äč‚Äčissue‚Äč‚Äčfiles‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčmake‚Äč
         ‚Äčtheir‚Äč ‚Äčpositive‚Äč‚Äčpay‚Äč‚Äčexception‚Äč‚Äčdecisions‚Äč‚Äčwithin‚Äč‚Äčtheir‚Äč‚ÄčBusiness‚Äč‚ÄčOnline‚Äč‚ÄčBanking‚Äč‚Äčor‚Äč‚ÄčBusiness‚Äč
        ‚ÄčMobiliti app.‚Äč


                                                            ‚Äč2‚Äč
‚Äč :/Bank Policies/Electronic Banking Policy‚Äč
Y
‚ÄčSeptember 2022‚Äč
    ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčPremium‚Äč‚ÄčPositive‚Äč‚ÄčPay-‚Äč‚ÄčClients‚Äč‚Äčhave‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčability‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčupload‚Äč‚Äčtheir‚Äč‚Äčcheck‚Äč‚Äčissue‚Äč‚Äčfiles,‚Äč‚Äčmonitor‚Äč
         ‚ÄčACH‚Äč ‚Äčactivity,‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčmake‚Äč ‚Äčtheir‚Äč ‚Äčpositive‚Äč ‚Äčpay‚Äč ‚Äčexception‚Äč ‚Äčdecisions‚Äč ‚Äčwithin‚Äč ‚Äčtheir‚Äč ‚ÄčBusiness‚Äč
        ‚ÄčOnline Banking.‚Äč

    ‚Äč‚óŹ‚Äč ‚ÄčACBT‚Äč‚ÄčCard‚Äč‚ÄčControl-‚Äč‚ÄčClients‚Äč‚Äčhave‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčability‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčregister‚Äč‚Äčtheir‚Äč‚Äčdebit‚Äč‚Äčcards‚Äč‚Äčvia‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčmobility‚Äč
         ‚Äčapp.‚Äč ‚ÄčOnce‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčcard‚Äč‚Äčis‚Äč‚Äčregistered‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčclient‚Äč‚Äčcould‚Äč‚Äčturn‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčcard‚Äč‚Äčon/off,‚Äč‚Äčset‚Äč‚Äčspecific‚Äč‚Äčlimits,‚Äč
          ‚Äčor‚Äč ‚Äčset‚Äč ‚Äčspecific‚Äč ‚Äčtransaction‚Äč ‚Äčtypes.‚Äč ‚ÄčThe‚Äč ‚Äčclient‚Äč ‚Äčsettings‚Äč ‚Äčin‚Äč ‚ÄčCard‚Äč ‚ÄčControl‚Äč ‚Äčdo‚Äč ‚Äčnot‚Äč ‚Äčoverride‚Äč
           ‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč ‚Äčdefined‚Äč ‚Äčcard‚Äč ‚Äčlimits‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčit‚Äč ‚Äčdoes‚Äč ‚Äčnot‚Äč ‚Äčbypass‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚ÄčEnFact‚Äč ‚Äčcard‚Äč ‚Äčmonitoring‚Äč
        ‚Äčprocess.‚Äč

‚ÄčASSESSMENT OF RISK TO THE INSTITUTION‚Äč

‚Äč utsourced‚Äč ‚Äčelectronic‚Äč ‚Äčbanking‚Äč ‚Äčservices‚Äč ‚Äčcan‚Äč ‚Äčcontribute‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčoperational‚Äč ‚Äčrisks‚Äč ‚Äč(also‚Äč ‚Äčreferred‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčas‚Äč
O
‚Äčtransaction‚Äč ‚Äčrisks).‚Äč ‚ÄčOperational‚Äč ‚Äčrisk‚Äč ‚Äčmay‚Äč ‚Äčarise‚Äč ‚Äčfrom‚Äč ‚Äčfraud,‚Äč ‚Äčerror,‚Äč ‚Äčor‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčinability‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčdeliver‚Äč
 ‚Äčproducts‚Äč ‚Äčor‚Äč ‚Äčservices,‚Äč ‚Äčmaintain‚Äč ‚Äča‚Äč ‚Äčcompetitive‚Äč ‚Äčposition,‚Äč ‚Äčor‚Äč ‚Äčmanage‚Äč ‚Äčinformation.‚Äč ‚ÄčIt‚Äč‚Äčexists‚Äč‚Äčin‚Äč‚Äčeach‚Äč
  ‚Äčprocess‚Äč ‚Äčinvolved‚Äč ‚Äčin‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčdelivery‚Äč ‚Äčof‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äôs‚Äč ‚Äčproducts‚Äč‚Äčor‚Äč‚Äčservices.‚Äč‚ÄčOperational‚Äč‚Äčrisk‚Äč
   ‚Äčnot‚Äč ‚Äčonly‚Äč ‚Äčincludes‚Äč ‚Äčoperations‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčtransaction‚Äč ‚Äčprocessing,‚Äč ‚Äčbut‚Äč ‚Äčalso‚Äč ‚Äčareas‚Äč ‚Äčsuch‚Äč ‚Äčas‚Äč ‚Äčclient‚Äč ‚Äčservice,‚Äč
    ‚Äčsystems‚Äč ‚Äčdevelopment‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčsupport,‚Äč ‚Äčinternal‚Äč ‚Äčcontrol‚Äč ‚Äčprocesses,‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčcapacity‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčcontingency‚Äč
     ‚Äčplanning.‚Äč‚ÄčOperational‚Äč‚Äčrisk‚Äč‚Äčalso‚Äč‚Äčmay‚Äč‚Äčaffect‚Äč‚Äčother‚Äč‚Äčrisks‚Äč‚Äčsuch‚Äč‚Äčas‚Äč‚Äčinterest‚Äč‚Äčrate,‚Äč‚Äčcompliance,‚Äč‚Äčliquidity,‚Äč
      ‚Äčprice, strategic, or reputation risk.‚Äč

‚Äč lthough‚Äč‚Äčnew‚Äč‚Äčtechnology‚Äč‚Äčhas‚Äč‚Äčprovided‚Äč‚Äčimportant‚Äč‚Äčbenefits‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčits‚Äč‚Äčclients,‚Äč
A
‚Äčit‚Äč ‚Äčalso‚Äč ‚Äčexposes‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčnew‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčdifferent‚Äč‚Äčrisks.‚Äč‚ÄčAs‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč‚Äčincreases‚Äč
 ‚Äčits‚Äč ‚Äčdependency‚Äč ‚Äčon‚Äč ‚Äčtechnology‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčdeliver‚Äč ‚Äčservices‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčprocess‚Äč ‚Äčinformation,‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčrisk‚Äč ‚Äčof‚Äč ‚Äčadverse‚Äč
  ‚Äčconsequences‚Äč ‚Äčfrom‚Äč ‚Äčoperational‚Äč ‚Äčfailure‚Äč ‚Äčincreases.‚Äč ‚ÄčThese‚Äč ‚Äčrisks‚Äč ‚Äčultimately‚Äč‚Äčthreaten‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčsafety‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč
   ‚Äčsoundness of the institution.‚Äč

‚Äč he‚Äč ‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč ‚Äčhas‚Äč ‚Äčidentified‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčdocumented‚Äč ‚Äča‚Äč ‚Äčcomprehensive‚Äč ‚Äčelectronic‚Äč ‚Äčbanking‚Äč ‚Äčrisk‚Äč
T
‚Äčassessment‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčinformation‚Äč ‚Äčtechnology‚Äč ‚Äčcyber‚Äč ‚Äčsecurity‚Äč ‚Äčpolicy.‚Äč ‚ÄčSince‚Äč ‚Äčtechnology‚Äč ‚Äčgrowth‚Äč ‚Äčis‚Äč ‚Äčnot‚Äč
 ‚Äčstatic,‚Äč‚Äčnew‚Äč‚Äčrisks‚Äč‚Äčare‚Äč‚Äčregularly‚Äč‚Äčcreated.‚Äč‚ÄčThe‚Äč‚Äčboard‚Äč‚Äčdirects‚Äč‚Äčexecutive‚Äč‚Äčmanagement‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčidentify‚Äč‚Äčrisks‚Äč
  ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč ‚Äčby‚Äč ‚Äčusing‚Äč ‚Äča‚Äč ‚Äčprocess‚Äč ‚Äčof‚Äč ‚Äčmonitoring,‚Äč ‚Äčmeasuring,‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčidentifying‚Äč ‚Äčrisks‚Äč ‚Äčfor‚Äč
   ‚Äčwhich the INSTITUTION must be prepared.‚Äč

‚Äč he‚Äč‚Äčboard‚Äč‚Äčappoints‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčassistant‚Äč‚Äčvice‚Äč‚Äčpresident‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčdeposit‚Äč‚Äčoperations‚Äč‚Äčas‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčindividual‚Äč‚Äčultimately‚Äč
T
‚Äčresponsible‚Äč ‚Äčfor‚Äč ‚Äčassessing‚Äč ‚Äčrisks‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčimplementing‚Äč ‚Äčprocedures‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčminimize‚Äč ‚Äčthose‚Äč ‚Äčrisks‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč
 ‚ÄčINSTITUTION.‚Äč ‚ÄčIt‚Äč ‚Äčalso‚Äč ‚Äčdirects‚Äč ‚Äčaudit‚Äč ‚Äčpersonnel‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčconduct‚Äč ‚Äčreviews‚Äč ‚Äčof‚Äč ‚Äčall‚Äč ‚Äčareas‚Äč ‚Äčof‚Äč ‚Äčelectronic‚Äč
  ‚Äčbanking‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčassess‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčinternal‚Äč‚Äčcontrol‚Äč‚Äčstructure‚Äč‚Äčput‚Äč‚Äčin‚Äč‚Äčplace‚Äč‚Äčby‚Äč‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč‚Äčmanagement‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč
   ‚Äčto verify that all INSTITUTION departments adhere to the general requirements of this policy.‚Äč

‚ÄčMITIGATION OF RISK AND RISK MANAGEMENT‚Äč

‚Äč he‚Äč ‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč ‚Äčincludes‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčfollowing‚Äč‚Äčelements‚Äč‚Äčin‚Äč‚Äčits‚Äč‚Äčelectronic‚Äč‚Äčbanking‚Äč‚Äčrisk‚Äč‚Äčmanagement‚Äč
T
‚Äčplan.‚Äč ‚ÄčFor‚Äč ‚Äčspecific‚Äč ‚Äčdetails‚Äč ‚Äčon‚Äč ‚Äčmanagement‚Äôs‚Äč ‚Äčassessment‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčmitigation‚Äč ‚Äčof‚Äč ‚ÄčIT‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčelectronic‚Äč
 ‚Äčbanking‚Äč ‚Äčrisks,‚Äč ‚Äčreference‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚ÄčElectronic‚Äč ‚ÄčBanking‚Äč ‚ÄčAuthentication‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚ÄčRisk‚Äč ‚ÄčAssessment‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč
  ‚ÄčInformation Technology Cyber Security Policy.‚Äč

                                                             ‚Äč3‚Äč
‚Äč :/Bank Policies/Electronic Banking Policy‚Äč
Y
‚ÄčSeptember 2022‚Äč
‚ÄčPlanning and Feasibility Analysis‚Äč

‚ÄčEnsure that each proposal for new electronic banking services will be analyzed in three stages:‚Äč

    ‚Äč‚Ė™‚Äč ‚ÄčStudy: Needs and objectives will be analyzed and alternatives developed.‚Äč

    ‚Äč‚Ė™‚Äč ‚ÄčDesign‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčdevelopment:‚Äč ‚ÄčThe‚Äč ‚Äčbest‚Äč ‚Äčsolution‚Äč ‚Äčis‚Äč ‚Äčidentified‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčinstalled;‚Äč ‚Äčpolicies‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč
        ‚Äčprocedures are developed; and documentation is completed.‚Äč
    ‚Äč‚Ė™‚Äč ‚ÄčOperation: The system is properly operated and maintained.‚Äč

‚Äč he‚Äč ‚ÄčProduct‚Äč ‚ÄčRisk‚Äč ‚ÄčAnalysis‚Äč ‚Äčform‚Äč ‚Äčwill‚Äč ‚Äčbe‚Äč ‚Äčprepared‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčpresented‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčour‚Äč ‚ÄčEnterprise‚Äč ‚ÄčRisk‚Äč
T
‚ÄčManagement Committee to thoroughly review all new electronic banking services.‚Äč


‚ÄčIncident Planning and Preparedness‚Äč

‚Äč he‚Äč ‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč ‚Äčmaintains‚Äč ‚Äča‚Äč‚Äčbusiness‚Äč‚Äčcontinuity‚Äč‚Äčplan,‚Äč‚Äčprocedures,‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčan‚Äč‚Äčincident‚Äč‚Äčresponse‚Äč
T
‚Äčplan‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčprepare‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč‚Äčfor‚Äč‚Äčrisks‚Äč‚Äčthat‚Äč‚Äčmay‚Äč‚Äčoccur‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčoutline‚Äč‚Äčaction‚Äč‚Äčplans‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčrespond‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč
 ‚Äčdisasters‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčincidents.‚Äč ‚ÄčThe‚Äč ‚Äčbusiness‚Äč ‚Äčcontinuity‚Äč‚Äčplan‚Äč‚Äčincludes‚Äč‚Äčregular‚Äč‚Äčtesting‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčkey‚Äč‚Äčelectronic‚Äč
  ‚Äčbanking services.‚Äč

‚ÄčInternal Routines and Controls‚Äč

‚Äč ank‚Äč‚Äčmanagement‚Äč‚Äčhas‚Äč‚Äčdeveloped‚Äč‚Äča‚Äč‚Äčrange‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčcomprehensive‚Äč‚Äčsystem‚Äč‚Äčreviews‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčtests‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčvalidate‚Äč
B
‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčcontrols‚Äč ‚Äčincorporated‚Äč ‚Äčinto‚Äč ‚Äčeach‚Äč‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč‚Äčarea‚Äč‚Äčfor‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčpurpose‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčprotecting‚Äč‚Äčhardware‚Äč
 ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčsoftware,‚Äč ‚Äčproprietary‚Äč ‚Äčdata,‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčelectronic‚Äč ‚Äčtransmissions.‚Äč ‚ÄčFurthermore,‚Äč ‚Äčmanagement‚Äč ‚Äčis‚Äč
  ‚Äčinstructed to train and maintain users on the need to adhere to control standards.‚Äč

‚ÄčManagement Supervision and Internal Controls‚Äč

‚Äč anagement‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčboard‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčdirectors‚Äč‚Äčare‚Äč‚Äčactively‚Äč‚Äčinvolved‚Äč‚Äčin‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčdecision-making‚Äč‚Äčprocesses‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč
M
‚Äčeffectively‚Äč‚Äčmitigate‚Äč‚Äčrisks.‚Äč‚ÄčThe‚Äč‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč‚Äčhas‚Äč‚Äčestablished‚Äč‚Äčcontrols‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčprocesses‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčmitigate‚Äč
 ‚Äčelectronic banking risks. In addition, the following oversight is in place:‚Äč

    1‚Äč .‚Äč ‚ÄčElectronic Banking Risk Assessment‚Äč
     ‚Äč2.‚Äč ‚ÄčIT Committee ‚Äď Quarterly meetings‚Äč
      ‚Äč3.‚Äč ‚ÄčIT Risk Assessment‚Äč
       ‚Äč4.‚Äč ‚ÄčIT Strategic Plan prepared annually‚Äč
        ‚Äč5.‚Äč ‚ÄčIT Audit ‚Äď Annual review and testing of controls & supervision‚Äč
         ‚Äč6.‚Äč ‚ÄčIT dashboard‚Äč
          ‚Äč7.‚Äč ‚ÄčDeposit Operations Dashboard‚Äč
           ‚Äč8.‚Äč ‚ÄčElectronic Banking Summary‚Äč



                                                        ‚Äč4‚Äč
‚Äč :/Bank Policies/Electronic Banking Policy‚Äč
Y
‚ÄčSeptember 2022‚Äč
‚Äč eports‚Äč ‚Äčare‚Äč ‚Äčprovided‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčboard‚Äč ‚Äčperiodically‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčdetail‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčactions‚Äč‚Äčtaken‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčassess‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčmanage‚Äč
R
‚Äčelectronic banking risks.‚Äč

‚ÄčAuthentication Controls‚Äč

‚Äč he‚Äč ‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč ‚Äčwill‚Äč ‚Äčrequire‚Äč ‚Äčuser‚Äč ‚Äčauthentication‚Äč ‚Äčcontrols‚Äč‚Äčon‚Äč‚Äčall‚Äč‚Äčelectronic‚Äč‚Äčbanking‚Äč‚Äčservices‚Äč
T
‚Äčused‚Äč ‚Äčby‚Äč ‚Äčclients.‚Äč ‚ÄčDifferent‚Äč ‚Äčdegrees‚Äč ‚Äčof‚Äč ‚Äčauthentication‚Äč‚Äčrequirements‚Äč‚Äčwill‚Äč‚Äčbe‚Äč‚Äčapplied‚Äč‚Äčin‚Äč‚Äčaccordance‚Äč
 ‚Äčwith‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčperceived‚Äč ‚Äčrisk.‚Äč ‚ÄčAttached‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčthis‚Äč ‚Äčpolicy‚Äč ‚Äčas‚Äč ‚ÄčExhibit‚Äč ‚ÄčA‚Äč ‚Äčare‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčcurrent‚Äč ‚Äčauthentication‚Äč
  ‚Äčcontrols utilized.‚Äč

‚Äč lient‚Äč‚ÄčEnrollment‚Äč‚ÄčAuthentication‚Äč‚ÄčControls‚Äč‚Äčare‚Äč‚Äčcovered‚Äč‚Äčin‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚ÄčElectronic‚Äč‚ÄčBanking‚Äč‚ÄčAuthentication‚Äč
C
‚Äčand Risk Assessment.‚Äč


‚ÄčOperations and procedures requiring Dual Control‚Äč

‚Äč he‚Äč‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč‚Äčwill‚Äč‚Äčdevelop‚Äč‚Äčinternal‚Äč‚Äčprocessing‚Äč‚Äčprocedures‚Äč‚Äčdesigned‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčmitigate‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčrisks‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč
T
‚Äčproviding‚Äč ‚Äčelectronic‚Äč ‚Äčbanking‚Äč ‚Äčservices. Electronic‚Äč ‚Äčprocesses‚Äč‚Äčwhich‚Äč‚Äčare‚Äč‚Äčhigher‚Äč‚Äčrisk‚Äč‚Äčin‚Äč‚Äčnature‚Äč‚Äčwill‚Äč
 ‚Äčrequire‚Äč ‚Äčdual‚Äč ‚Äčcontrols‚Äč ‚Äčor‚Äč ‚Äčenhanced‚Äč ‚Äčsystem‚Äč ‚Äčaccess‚Äč ‚Äčby‚Äč ‚Äčemployees. These‚Äč ‚Äčprocesses‚Äč ‚Äčhave‚Äč ‚Äčbeen‚Äč
  ‚Äčidentified and attached to this policy as Exhibit B.‚Äč
   ‚ÄčVendor Due Diligence‚Äč

‚Äč ue‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčspecialized‚Äč ‚Äčexpertise‚Äč ‚Äčneeded‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčdesign,‚Äč ‚Äčimplement,‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčservice‚Äč ‚Äčnew‚Äč ‚Äčtechnologies,‚Äč
D
‚Äčvendors‚Äč‚Äčare‚Äč‚Äčneeded‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčprovide‚Äč‚Äčresources‚Äč‚Äčthat‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚ÄčINSTITUTION‚Äč‚Äčis‚Äč‚Äčunable‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčprovide‚Äč‚Äčon‚Äč‚Äčits‚Äč‚Äčown.‚Äč
 ‚ÄčHowever,‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčboard‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčsenior‚Äč‚Äčmanagement‚Äč‚Äčremain‚Äč‚Äčresponsible‚Äč‚Äčfor‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčperformance‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčactions‚Äč
  ‚Äčof‚Äč ‚Äčthese‚Äč ‚Äčvendors‚Äč ‚Äčwhile‚Äč ‚Äčthey‚Äč ‚Äčare‚Äč ‚Äčperforming‚Äč ‚Äčwork‚Äč ‚Äčfor‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚ÄčINSTITUTION.‚Äč ‚ÄčManagement‚Äč ‚Äčwill‚Äč
   ‚Äčperform‚Äč ‚Äčdue‚Äč ‚Äčdiligence‚Äč ‚Äčon‚Äč ‚Äčthese‚Äč ‚Äčvendors‚Äč ‚Äčas‚Äč ‚Äčoutlined‚Äč ‚Äčin‚Äč ‚Äčdetail‚Äč ‚Äčin‚Äč ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚ÄčInformation‚Äč ‚ÄčTechnology‚Äč
    ‚ÄčCyber Security Policy.‚Äč

‚Äč he‚Äč‚Äčelectronic‚Äč‚Äčbanking‚Äč‚Äčchannels‚Äč‚Äčare‚Äč‚Äčhosted‚Äč‚Äčat‚Äč‚ÄčFiserv‚Äč‚ÄčJohns‚Äč‚ÄčCreek.‚Äč ‚ÄčPart‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčannual‚Äč‚Äčvendor‚Äč‚Äčdue‚Äč
T
‚Äčdiligence‚Äč‚Äčcompleted‚Äč‚Äčon‚Äč‚ÄčFiserv‚Äč‚Äčis‚Äč‚Äča‚Äč‚Äčreview‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčtheir‚Äč‚ÄčSOC1‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčtheir‚Äč‚Äčrequirements‚Äč‚Äčfor‚Äč‚Äčencryption‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč
 ‚Äčdata communications.‚Äč

‚ÄčExpertise and Training‚Äč

‚ÄčEmployee Education:‚Äč

I‚Äč t‚Äč‚Äčis‚Äč‚Äčcritical‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčthe‚Äč‚Äčsuccess‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äča‚Äč‚Äčrisk‚Äč‚Äčmanagement‚Äč‚Äčprogram‚Äč‚Äčthat‚Äč‚Äčkey‚Äč‚Äčemployees‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčvendors‚Äč‚Äčhave‚Äč
 ‚Äčthe‚Äč ‚Äčexpertise‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčskills‚Äč ‚Äčnecessary‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč ‚Äčperform‚Äč ‚Äčcritical‚Äč ‚Äčfunctions,‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčthat‚Äč ‚Äčthey‚Äč ‚Äčare‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčremain‚Äč
  ‚Äčproperly‚Äč ‚Äčtrained.‚Äč ‚ÄčManagement‚Äč ‚Äčwill‚Äč‚Äčallocate‚Äč‚Äčsufficient‚Äč‚Äčresources‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčhire‚Äč‚Äčand‚Äč‚Äčmaintain‚Äč‚Äčemployee‚Äč
   ‚Äčtraining‚Äč ‚Äčand‚Äč ‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčensure‚Äč‚Äčthat‚Äč‚Äčadequate‚Äč‚Äčbackup‚Äč‚Äčexists‚Äč‚Äčif‚Äč‚Äčcritical‚Äč‚Äčpeople‚Äč‚Äčleave.‚Äč‚ÄčTraining‚Äč‚Äčwill‚Äč‚Äčinclude‚Äč
    ‚Äčtechnical‚Äč ‚Äčcourse‚Äč ‚Äčwork,‚Äč ‚Äčattendance‚Äč ‚Äčat‚Äč ‚Äčindustry‚Äč ‚Äčconferences,‚Äč ‚Äčparticipation‚Äč ‚Äčin‚Äč ‚Äčindustry‚Äč ‚Äčworking‚Äč
     ‚Äčgroups,‚Äč‚Äčas‚Äč‚Äčwell‚Äč‚Äčas‚Äč‚Äčtime‚Äč‚Äčallotments‚Äč‚Äčfor‚Äč‚Äčappropriate‚Äč‚Äčstaff‚Äč‚Äčto‚Äč‚Äčkeep‚Äč‚Äčabreast‚Äč‚Äčof‚Äč‚Äčimportant‚Äč‚Äčtechnological‚Äč
      ‚Äčand market developments.‚Äč

 ‚Äčwith the INSTITUTION‚Äôs existing technology and will include vendors in the testing process.‚Äč
